root_text.sn51.21:1.2: “Pubbeva me, bhikkhave, sambodhā anabhisambuddhassa bodhisattasseva sato etadahosi:
root_text.sn51.21:1.3: ‘ko nu kho maggo, kā paṭipadā iddhipādabhāvanāyā’ti?
root_text.sn51.21:1.6: iti me chando na ca atilīno bhavissati, na ca atippaggahito bhavissati, na ca ajjhattaṁ saṅkhitto bhavissati, na ca bahiddhā vikkhitto bhavissati.
root_text.sn51.21:1.8: yathā pure tathā pacchā, yathā pacchā tathā pure;
root_text.sn51.21:1.9: yathā adho tathā uddhaṁ, yathā uddhaṁ tathā adho;
root_text.sn51.21:1.10: yathā divā tathā rattiṁ, yathā rattiṁ tathā divā.
root_text.sn51.21:1.11: Iti vivaṭena cetasā apariyonaddhena sappabhāsaṁ cittaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.sn51.21:1.15: iti me vīmaṁsā na ca atilīnā bhavissati, na ca atippaggahitā bhavissati, na ca ajjhattaṁ saṅkhittā bhavissati, na ca bahiddhā vikkhittā bhavissati.
root_text.sn51.21:1.17: yathā pure tathā pacchā, yathā pacchā tathā pure;
root_text.sn51.21:1.18: yathā adho tathā uddhaṁ, yathā uddhaṁ tathā adho;
root_text.sn51.21:1.19: yathā divā tathā rattiṁ yathā rattiṁ tathā divā’—
root_text.sn51.21:1.20: iti vivaṭena cetasā apariyonaddhena sappabhāsaṁ cittaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.sn51.21:2.1: Evaṁ bhāvitesu kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu catūsu iddhipādesu evaṁ bahulīkatesu anekavihitaṁ iddhividhaṁ paccanubhoti—ekopi hutvā bahudhā hoti, bahudhāpi hutvā eko hoti …pe… yāva brahmalokāpi kāyena vasaṁ vatteti.
root_text.sn51.21:2.2: Evaṁ bhāvitesu kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu catūsu iddhipādesu evaṁ bahulīkatesu, āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharatī”ti.

translation_text.sn51.21:1.2: “Mendicants, before my awakening—when I was still unawakened but intent on awakening—I thought:
translation_text.sn51.21:1.3: ‘What’s the path and practice for developing the bases of psychic power?’
translation_text.sn51.21:1.5: ‘It’s when a mendicant develops the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to enthusiasm, and active effort.
translation_text.sn51.21:1.6: They think: “My enthusiasm won’t be too lax or too tense. And it’ll be neither constricted internally nor scattered externally.”
translation_text.sn51.21:1.7: And they meditate perceiving before and behind:
translation_text.sn51.21:1.8: as before, so behind; as behind, so before;
translation_text.sn51.21:1.9: as below, so above; as above, so below;
translation_text.sn51.21:1.10: as by day, so by night; as by night, so by day.
translation_text.sn51.21:1.11: And so, with an open and unenveloped heart, they develop a mind that’s full of radiance.
translation_text.sn51.21:1.12: They develop the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to energy …
translation_text.sn51.21:1.15: They think: “My inquiry won’t be too lax or too tense. And it’ll be neither constricted internally nor scattered externally.”
translation_text.sn51.21:1.16: And they meditate perceiving before and behind:
translation_text.sn51.21:1.17: as before, so behind; as behind, so before;
translation_text.sn51.21:1.18: as below, so above; as above, so below;
translation_text.sn51.21:1.19: as by day, so by night; as by night, so by day.
translation_text.sn51.21:1.20: And so, with an open and unenveloped heart, they develop a mind that’s full of radiance.’
translation_text.sn51.21:2.1: When the four bases of psychic power have been developed and cultivated in this way, a mendicant wields the many kinds of psychic power: multiplying themselves and becoming one again … controlling the body as far as the realm of divinity.
translation_text.sn51.21:2.2: When the four bases of psychic power have been developed and cultivated in this way, they realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. And they live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.”
translation_text.sn51.21:2.4: (The six direct knowledges should be told in full.)
